newsArray.title: Justin Langer: Don’t downplay what your achievements in an age where we’re told to keep chasing the next goal
newsArray.description: The biggest mistake a sportsperson can make is to look over their shoulder, but here is a difference between living in the past and learning from your achievements, says Justin Langer.
creator: the west australian

newsArray.title: Matthew Hollard's dream debut sees Ireland stun World Champions India
newsArray.description: Debutant seamer Matthew Hollard was named Player of the Match for his 3/28 figures in Ireland's historic first-ever international win against India. The hosts defended 183, restricting India to 148 to take a 1-0 lead in the T20I series.
creator: asianet news central

newsArray.title: Nicholls’ drop defines the day as Duckett cashes in on luck to deliver for England | Andy Bull
newsArray.description: On an unforgiving Trent Bridge pitch where the runs flow like melted butter, every little mistake in the field is measured in centuries The stands around Trent Bridge were looking pretty thin by the time England’s opening batters made it out to the middle. Not that there were any spare tickets going, the first three days of the game are sold out, only plenty of the people who had bought them had beaten a retreat to the shady parts round the back of the ground. It was a pitiless day for cricket, and the back alleys were packed with people who had been defeated by the heat. They were stooped over the railings of the stairways, slouched along the walls of the indoor school and slumped in the shadow of the Radcliffe Road Stand. There’s no shelter out in the middle. This is a hard Test, played for high stakes on an unforgiving wicket and under intense scrutiny. Some matches are defined by the deeds people achieve in them, others by the mistakes they make during them. And on a pitch like this, where the runs flow like melted butter, everyone watching is glaringly aware of every last little mistake made on the field. The errors are being measured in centuries. Continue reading...
creator: andy bull

newsArray.description: June 26 (Reuters) - ⁠New Zealand bowler Blair Tickner has ⁠been ruled out of the ‌rest of the third test match against England after suffering concussion, the team said on ​Friday.

newsArray.title: Ireland Secures Historic Victory Over India in T20I Series Opener
newsArray.description: In a remarkable turn of events, Ireland defeated India by 34 runs in the first T20I match, marking their first victory over the World Champions in international cricket. Captain Lorcan Tucker hailed the win as 'pretty special,' highlighting key performances from debutants Matthew Hollard and Jai Moondra. With this victory, Ireland aims to secure the series in the upcoming match. The excitement builds as the team prepares for another showdown against India, with hopes of achieving a historic series win at home.
creator: narendra chaudhary
